steps.title: Use the scale shortcut: equal ratios from similar triangles
steps.content:
Both trees cast shadows at the same sun angle, forming similar right triangles. Height-to-shadow ratio is constant:
$$\frac{\text{height}}{\text{shadow}} = \frac{10}{5} = 2$$

steps.title: Apply the ratio to tree 2
steps.content:
Tree 2 has a shadow of 2 ft, so its height $h = 2 \times 2 = 4$ ft.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
